findings.evidence: `mathlib/Mathlib/NumberTheory/MahlerMeasure.lean:55` defines `Polynomial.boxPoly : Set ℤ[X] := {p | p.natDegree ≤ n ∧ ∀ i, B₁ i ≤ p.coeff i ∧ p.coeff i ≤ B₂ i}`, and `:57` proves `ncard_boxPoly`; the new `intPolynomialBox` is the constant-bound `[-C,C]` instance, with high coefficients handled by `natDegree ≤ D`.
findings.fix: Use or explicitly relate to `Polynomial.boxPoly D (fun _ => -(C : ℝ)) (fun _ => (C : ℝ))`, and derive the count/finiteness from `Polynomial.ncard_boxPoly` or the existing TauCeti count rather than adding an independent box API.
findings.issue: `intPolynomialBox` is a special case of Mathlib's existing integer coefficient box API, so this introduces a parallel polynomial-box definition for the same bounded-degree/bounded-coefficient family.

findings.evidence: `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Polynomial/Degree/Operations.lean:93` proves `theorem ext_iff_natDegree_le {p q : R[X]} {n : ℕ} (hp : p.natDegree ≤ n) (hq : q.natDegree ≤ n) : p = q ↔ ∀ i, i ≤ n → p.coeff i = q.coeff i`; TauCeti already uses it for the same injection at `code/TauCeti/Algebra/Polynomial/CardBoundedCoeff.lean:64`.
findings.fix: Delete this public theorem or make it private/local, and prove the finite wrapper directly with `Polynomial.ext_iff_natDegree_le hp.1 hq.1`.
findings.issue: `coeffVec_injOn` exports a parallel extensionality/injectivity lemma for bounded-degree polynomials that is already covered by `Polynomial.ext_iff_natDegree_le`.

summary: The remaining additions are mostly namespace-specific wrappers around existing polynomial box/extensionality APIs. Reuse the existing Mathlib/TauCeti statements instead of exporting parallel public declarations.
